claimText: In 1947, India wanted it to be settled according to international principles of maritime law, called the Thalweg Principle (boundary can be fixed only in the middle of the navigable channel) in 1947.
evidenceExcerpt: In 1947, India wanted it to be settled according to international principles of maritime law, called the Thalweg Principle (boundary can be fixed only in the middle of the navigable channel) in 1947.
examUse: Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.

claimText: - Pakistan, however, claimed that Sir Creek was not navigable, so the dispute could not be settled according to the Thalweg principle.
evidenceExcerpt: - Pakistan, however, claimed that Sir Creek was not navigable, so the dispute could not be settled according to the Thalweg principle.

claimText: ### About Commission on the Limits of the Continental Shelf (CLCS)

examUse: Could support a statement-based or institution-feature question.

claimText: - CLCS meets at the UN Headquarters in New York.

claimText: - Objective: To facilitate implementation of the United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea, 1982 in respect of the establishment of the outer limits of the continental shelf beyond 200 nautical miles beyond baseline.
